operations.value: Most clinical exercise studies test Rhodiola root or root-and-rhizome extracts, often standardized near 3% total rosavins and 1% salidroside, but extraction methods and marker reporting vary and some trials omit composition details; findings should not be generalized to unverified powders or differently standardized products.
operations.notes: Adds product-generalizability context using the formulations and reporting limitations described in the exercise review literature.

sources.title: Effects of Rhodiola Rosea Supplementation on Exercise and Sport: A Systematic Review
Rhodiola rosea supplementation on sports performance: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials

notes: Prevents evidence from standardized extracts being silently transferred to any Rhodiola powder or commercial preparation.
